0024c0284321b7e1.summary: Discover the MOTAT collection like never before during this unique behind-the-scenes tour.
0024c0284321b7e1.description: This is a unique opportunity to explore the MOTAT collection from behind the scenes. Attendees will get an exclusive look at the museum's vast holdings, discovering items and areas usually kept private from the general public. Don't miss these special extra dates for an unparalleled deep dive into the collection.

021f448b54049758.summary: Dr Josh Emmitt discusses the daily life of a museum archaeologist, covering everything from digging into the past to challenging misconceptions.
021f448b54049758.description: Digging into the past, excavating new information and challenging misconceptions is all in a day's work of a museum archaeologist. Dr Josh Emmitt, Curator Archaeology at Auckland Museum, takes attendees through his daily journey of discovery during this special talk for New Zealand Archaeology Week.

034e0f1e13de5956.summary: Commemorate ANZAC Day with dawn service, tours, performances.
034e0f1e13de5956.description: This Anzac Day, Auckland Museum hosts a day of commemoration featuring the Dawn Service, free guided tours, choral performances, and poetry readings. Entry to the museum is complimentary for all visitors on this day. The museum opens immediately after the Dawn Service.

0625a85ce3dcc2fd.summary: This talk reveals the history and secrets of the iconic Clock Tower.
0625a85ce3dcc2fd.description: The Clock Tower offers a tangible link to Auckland's past. This presentation traces the development and significance of this architectural landmark over the last century. Attendees will gain insight into the engineering, cultural milestones, and stories embodied by this central city feature.

07ddca6f970286a6.summary: This workshop offers a nurturing journey into Siva Samoa, where movement supports wellbeing.
07ddca6f970286a6.description: Participants explore foundational Siva Samoa steps and their cultural meanings—gestures that honour connections to land, ocean, sky, ancestry, and community. Through soft hands, grounded feet, and mindful breath, attendees experience how movement mirrors the natural world. Rooted in traditional practice, the session invites people to slow down, reconnect, and honour the body. Open to all levels.

0b472d46885f049a.summary: Professor Judy Bowen addresses the reliability, usability, and safety of technology embedded in modern daily life.
0b472d46885f049a.description: This talk examines the pervasive nature of technology in contemporary life. It addresses critical questions surrounding how to ensure that these deeply embedded systems remain reliable, usable, and fundamentally safe for all users. The discussion explores the societal challenges posed by technological dependency.

0c5a2aaa12aaa6f5.summary: Professor John Dudley will give an overview of photonics, detailing the interplay between basic science and innovation.
0c5a2aaa12aaa6f5.description: This talk provides an overview of photonics, exploring this enabling technology. It will detail the critical interplay that exists between fundamental basic science research and practical innovation within the field. Join us at the University of Auckland for this insightful presentation.

0ce3b2985b99250e.summary: Explore Māori Io traditions, focusing on 'the Soul of Things' and moving beyond the concept of a Supreme God.
0ce3b2985b99250e.description: This talk explores Māori Io traditions, examining the concept of 'the Soul of Things' and discussing the depth of belief beyond a singular Supreme God. Dr Deane Galbraith, a Senior Lecturer in Religion/Mātai Whairuataka at the University of Otago/Ōtākou Whakaihu WakaFew, will present these discussions.

0ce9fae1b4d99995.summary: Panel discussion on diaspora communities navigating global tensions.
0ce9fae1b4d99995.description: The NZIIA Wellington Branch Student Committee invites you to a panel discussion about the impact of rising global tensions on diaspora communities. Featuring Dr Kate McMillan and Dhilum Nightingale, this event will explore how geopolitical shifts affect migration, identity, and cross-border connections globally and in New Zealand.

0d0d2141e1bc8a11.summary: Tāmaki Makaurau musicians Ersha Island perform an intimate concert to celebrate the closing day of the exhibition Dreaming from afar.
0d0d2141e1bc8a11.description: Tāmaki Makaurau musicians Ersha Island will provide an intimate performance within the gallery, celebrating the closing day of the current exhibition, Dreaming from afar.

0f1ea4588f7224e7.summary: WEN Outreach Workshop on earthquake safety for young women.
0f1ea4588f7224e7.description: A free half-day workshop for Year 9 and 10 female students focusing on earthquake preparedness through creative problem-solving activities in engineering. Includes a complimentary pizza lunch.

1092d277ff445319.summary: Deloitte presents a look at building Aotearoa's future using data, featuring Databricks and Tech Women.
1092d277ff445319.description: This event focuses on building Aotearoa's future through the lens of data, featuring insights from Databricks and Tech Women. The event is hosted by Deloitte Techweek26.

11dc8475abd89d26.summary: Tamariki Time workshop at Te Papa, making nature picture frames.
11dc8475abd89d26.description: Engage in creative activities at Tamariki Time: Slow Burn – Ahi Tāmau. Create your own nature picture frame using native plants from Bush City.

184d6aaa593d1460.summary: Professor Alexandra Andhov discusses 'Big Tech: Too Big To Fail?' in her inaugural lecture.
184d6aaa593d1460.description: Inaugural Professorial Lecture by Alexandra Andhov addressing the role and impact of big tech companies. The discussion delves into whether these entities are too large to fail, challenging the status quo of digital dominance.

189671d66f52d283.summary: Explore siapo-making as a living practice through talanoa.
189671d66f52d283.description: This two-day workshop introduces participants to the traditional art practice of siapo-making as a living practice. Through talanoa, we will explore the history of siapo in Sāmoa, its cultural significance and the ways it continues to be reimagined today.

19b443dab561d5b4.summary: Celebrate New Zealand Sign Language Week with whānau-friendly activities on Rongomaraeroa, Te Papa’s marae.
19b443dab561d5b4.description: Celebrate New Zealand Sign Language Week with whānau-friendly activities on Rongomaraeroa, Te Papa’s marae.

1e871946e014fdb7.summary: Celebrate the announcement of the 2026 Ockham New Zealand Book Awards longlist, featuring readings from authors in all four categories.

1f6536ed97b55103.summary: 2026 WSBG Climate Festival Fortnight at Victoria University
1f6536ed97b55103.description: Wellington School of Business and Government (WSBG) at Te Herenga Waka—Victoria University of Wellington supports Wellington City Council’s first climate festival. Register here for more information.

2006b7e3c699fb83.summary: Professor Jonathan Barrett will discuss respecting equal human dignity: taxation and commercial law in the human rights state.
2006b7e3c699fb83.description: Professor Barrett's research concerns the practical application of human rights, particularly focusing on respecting equal human dignity through taxation and commercial law in the human rights state.

200da7812a57519d.summary: Lucy O’Hagan performs her acclaimed memoir, Everything But the Medicine: A Doctor’s Tale.
200da7812a57519d.description: This reading brings Lucy O’Hagan’s acclaimed memoir, Everything But the Medicine: A Doctor’s Tale, to life through performance. The work offers an intimate look into a doctor’s experiences as chronicled by O’Hagan. Audience members will engage with the narrative that frames her professional life and personal reflections.

212868d681362f71.summary: Explore Yongchun lacquer baskets and paint your own.
212868d681362f71.description: Discover the beauty of Yongchun Lacquer Baskets, a traditional craft from Fujian blending weaving and lacquer art. Learn about its cultural story and create your own lacquer basket under expert guidance.

21be6b77ec2e071a.summary: Examines Southeast Asia's critical juncture as world order fragments amid Trump's tariffs and the South China Sea.
21be6b77ec2e071a.description: As world order fragments, Southeast Asia finds itself at a critical juncture. The lecture will address the impact of President Trump’s sweeping tariff agenda and transactional approach to allies on the region.

22b6c643fdffc426.summary: Professor Papaarangi Reid leads a wānanga-style discussion on the ongoing health inequities faced by Māori communities and the system changes required for wellbeing.
22b6c643fdffc426.description: This discussion examines the persistent health inequities confronting Māori communities. Participants will engage with the complex issues necessitating genuine, systemic change within the health sector to improve wellbeing outcomes. The conversation aims to foster a deep understanding of current disparities and pathways toward equity.

22f56ba953bbd60d.summary: Professor Jonathan Barrett presents his inaugural lecture on the practical application of human rights in taxation and commercial law within a human rights state.

speakers.name: Professor Jonathan Barrett
speakers.bio: Professor at Victoria University, focusing on the practical application of human rights.

2411337464843d25.summary: Explore Samoan female tattooing as cultural knowledge archive in a workshop by Grounded Rosanna.
2411337464843d25.description: This workshop centres the malu, the Samoan female tattoo, as a living archive of cultural knowledge and ancestral presence. Through discussion, drawing, writing, and embodied practice, participants will explore how Indigenous knowledge is encoded in the body, and how these markings intersect with gender, identity, and sovereignty. Grounded Rosanna's own malu and artistic practice inform the workshop's critical reflection on issues of cultural appropriation, authorship, and the responsibilities of working with Indigenous visual systems.

274567a260b0d69b.summary: Thailand, often referred to as “The Land of Smiles,” is known for its rich cultural heritage, striking temples, world-renowned cuisine, and welcoming communities.
274567a260b0d69b.description: Thailand, often referred to as “The Land of Smiles,” boasts a rich cultural heritage, striking temples, world-renowned cuisine, and welcoming communities. This introductory course is designed for complete beginners and those preparing to travel to Thailand, offering a meaningful way to experience the culture more deeply.
274567a260b0d69b.exclude_reason: Focuses on language learning, not discussion/talk.

27acf592ab1ec661.summary: Conference exploring critical reflections on development practices.
27acf592ab1ec661.description: This year’s theme, Rebalancing Worlds – Reworking Practice, invites us to reflect critically on development practice and explore pathways to more just and equitable futures in the field of international development. Organized by Victoria University's DEVNET program.

283045f10f790cd0.summary: Deloitte Techweek26 hosts an event exploring the future landscape of open insurance within Aotearoa New Zealand.
283045f10f790cd0.description: This seminar, hosted by Deloitte Techweek26, explores the future of open insurance across Aotearoa New Zealand. The event will take place at Deloitte Wellington on Tuesday, May 19th, 2026.

29e57fcd6effffcd.summary: Queers & Wares is a market celebrating queer creativity, community, and connection. Browse unique handmade pieces, vintage clothing, and art from local artists.

2cb1e777f13f1b20.summary: This webinar examines the concept of neurotypicality and explores alternative perspectives on the neurodivergent life experience.
2cb1e777f13f1b20.description: The discussion challenges conventional understandings of 'normal' by centering on the neurodivergent experience. Attendees will gain insight into how society constructs definitions of typical functioning and how these definitions impact individual identities. The session offers a framework for rethinking what constitutes a 'normal' life.

2cff4af0fa8b324e.summary: Professor Hella von Unger presents research in education.
2cff4af0fa8b324e.description: Join the School of Education for a seminar featuring visiting Professor Hella von Unger from Munich. Professor von Unger will discuss her latest research findings in educational studies. The event includes refreshments and networking opportunities.

2eea4335227e348d.summary: Dr Stephen Skalicky discusses the intersection of literal, figurative language, and Large Language Models.
2eea4335227e348d.description: The School of Linguistics and Applied Language Studies hosts weekly seminars featuring a selection of visiting researchers as well as those from the. Presented by Dr Stephen Skalicky.

2ef8a4ebd6343108.summary: Mandarin Corner celebrates Mid-Autumn Festival with Chinese legends and traditions.
2ef8a4ebd6343108.description: Celebrate the Mid-Autumn Festival and explore one of China’s most beloved traditions. Discover the legend of Chang’e, learn about mooncakes and festival customs. Hosted by Victoria University's Mandarin Corner.

2f6c979a89a98d02.summary: Shaun McRae and Frank Wolak discuss capacity mechanisms as tools for ensuring electricity demand is met in renewable-dominated markets.
2f6c979a89a98d02.description: Capacity mechanisms are an increasingly popular tool for ensuring that demand can be met as electricity generation becomes dominated by renewable sources. The authors explore the implications of these mechanisms for grid reliability.

310f8cfdd19d4d16.summary: The sixth 'This Thinking Life' webinar explores the complex intersection of generative AI, the challenges of contaminated information, and rhubarb.
310f8cfdd19d4d16.description: The session examines the interplay between rapidly developing generative AI technologies and the subsequent proliferation of contaminated or misleading information across digital platforms. Participants will gain insight into critical evaluation skills necessary to navigate this complex information landscape, using rhubarb as a tangible metaphor for systemic breakdown or unexpected growth.

3119f8036c8cc821.summary: A panel discussion exploring aerial mapping technology held at the MOTAT Aviation Hall.
3119f8036c8cc821.description: Join MOTAT for an evening panel discussion in the Aviation Hall. Attendees will explore the latest developments and applications within aerial mapping technology. This promises an engaging conversation for aviation enthusiasts and industry professionals alike.

3139979a01b04c3d.summary: This two-day workshop introduces participants to the traditional art practice of siapo-making as a living practice, exploring its history and cultural significance.
3139979a01b04c3d.description: This two-day workshop introduces participants to the traditional art practice of siapo-making as a living practice. Through talanoa, participants will explore the history of siapo in Sāmoa, its cultural significance, and the ways it continues to be reimagined today.

3755a9be782fce30.summary: Victoria's Mandarin Corner explores Tai Chi basics.
3755a9be782fce30.description: Victoria University hosts a Mandarin Corner session focused on Tai Chi, inviting participants to practice this traditional Chinese martial art. The event combines language immersion with cultural experience through guided Tai Chi movements.

39f8376b33a79ab2.summary: Archivists discuss new discoveries at the Alexander Turnbull Library.
39f8376b33a79ab2.description: Archivists and historians unveil recently catalogued items from the Alexander Turnbull Library collection, featuring rare photographs and correspondence. These findings shed light on New Zealand's past and contribute to historical research.

3aba32013f4fbb71.description: Fast Forward 2026: Transcolonisation! Semester 1 In the Architecture NZ article Transcolonisation 1990-2020 , Anthony Hōete charted how architecture in Aotearoa was negotiating the complex entanglement of colonial inheritance and Indigenous resurgence. He described a 30-year trajectory in which design culture shifts from bicultural acknowledgement toward a more assertive Te Ao Māori architectural project, seeking to reconcile European traditions with Indigenous spatial knowledge.

3dff430382117182.summary: The inaugural 'This Thinking Life' webinar explores the complex intersection of Indigeneity and Artificial Intelligence.
3dff430382117182.description: This webinar initiates the 'This Thinking Life' series by examining the crucial intersection between Indigenous knowledge systems and the rapidly advancing field of Artificial Intelligence. Participants will gain insight into the unique ethical, cultural, and technological challenges presented by this convergence.

40f85f794ad4a481.summary: This lecture explores what happens when disability is viewed as a powerful cultural lens rather than merely a problem to solve.
40f85f794ad4a481.description: What happens when disability is seen as a powerful cultural lens, not a problem to solve? Join fellow AUT graduates for this insightful Great Graduates Speaker Series event.

47b8c89e63f1e5a3.summary: A creative workshop using printmaking and mindfulness to explore the familiar paths that bring a sense of comfort and connection to our daily routines.

47bff4f0c2afd6c2.summary: Examines the intersection of Artificial Intelligence with human communication in this webinar.
47bff4f0c2afd6c2.description: The webinar explores the evolving relationship between artificial intelligence and human methods of communication. Participants will gain insight into how AI technologies are reshaping interpersonal and mediated exchanges in modern life. This discussion addresses the practical implications of AI in communication theory and practice.

481fca738bd99f86.summary: Mark the closing of the exhibition He Kaupapa Waka with an afternoon of talks.
481fca738bd99f86.description: The closing of the exhibition He Kaupapa Waka features an afternoon of talks marking the end of the display. This gathering offers attendees a rare opportunity to connect with the themes explored in the exhibition through discussion. The event serves as a concluding reflection on the cultural and historical significance of the waka.

4dfb6447940ce651.summary: The lecture explores how Asia-Pacific's LNG trends can assist New Zealand in maintaining a stable and strong energy sector.
4dfb6447940ce651.description: This presentation examines the current trends surrounding Liquefied Natural Gas (LNG) across the Asia-Pacific region. Attendees will gain insight into critical energy balancing strategies that New Zealand can adopt from international examples. The discussion illuminates the interplay between regional energy dynamics and national energy security.

52cbd11960f48258.summary: Shaun Higgins discusses WWII POW art.
52cbd11960f48258.description: Curator Shaun Higgins explores how an Australian POW in WWII created remarkable portraits within a prison camp. Learn about the ingenuity and resilience of these artists during wartime adversity.

541dc5ec63ddc292.summary: Clinton Duffy, Curator of Marine Biology, guides attendees through the marine collections before the Sharks exhibition travels overseas, allowing close encounters with specimens like the Hammerhead Shark.
541dc5ec63ddc292.description: Before the Sharks exhibition heads overseas, join Clinton Duffy, Curator of Marine Biology, to explore the marine collections at Auckland Museum. Attendees will take a guided look into the world of sharks from their seats, and get to meet unique specimens, including the Hammerhead Shark.

543b9068d04b75b5.summary: The Weaponisation of Economics and How we Might Respond: The Art of Economic
543b9068d04b75b5.description: The Weaponisation of Economics and How we Might Respond: The Art of Economic

5506b567e7f48ff1.summary: Explore ideas for a high-performing health system for New Zealand, featuring insights from world-leading expert Dr Ezekiel Emanuel.
5506b567e7f48ff1.description: This summit will move beyond simplistic rankings to identify and discuss the core components of world-class health systems, drawing from comparative studies of 11 nations. Keynote speaker Dr Ezekiel Emanuel is a foremost health policy expert and author of Which Country Has The World’s Best Health Care?

575e90640f923cd8.summary: Discover how science and creativity coincide in a cyanotype workshop, experimenting with natural materials to create unique prints.
575e90640f923cd8.description: Discover how science and creativity coincide in this cyanotype workshop. Experiment with exposure, shadow, and UV light to create your own unique print using natural materials gathered from your surroundings. Lulu will guide participants through two hours of playful exploration, learning, and chats, perfect for anyone keen to try something new in a relaxed environment.

5b4464df305add17.summary: Professor Jennifer Miles-Chan delivers her Inaugural Lecture exploring lessons found at the intersection of metabolism, academia, and life.
5b4464df305add17.description: This inaugural lecture by Professor Jennifer Miles-Chan examines the profound connections between our metabolic processes, the structure of academia, and the broader scope of life. The talk suggests that focusing on the seemingly small details—the 'little things'—reveals significant underlying principles applicable across these disparate fields.

5e031be2b6ffbc07.summary: Explore the art of Bengali embroidery in a hands-on, communal session led by textile and community artist Sahana Rahman.

speakers.name: Sahana Rahman
speakers.bio: Textile and community artist specializing in Bengali embroidery.

600ca550291d52a4.summary: Professor Nicola Daly examines the surprising power of picturebooks in reflecting cultural values passed down to future generations.
600ca550291d52a4.description: Picturebooks hold significant cultural weight, reflecting what communities wish to transmit to their descendants (tamariki-mokopuna). Understanding these narratives reveals much about the values and knowledge systems being passed down through generations. This exploration illuminates how seemingly simple books carry profound cultural messages.

61b44da4b6ece3fd.summary: Join us this Archaeology Week for a family-friendly event offering a unique opportunity to become an archaeologist for the day! Explore the fascinating world of uncovering history.
61b44da4b6ece3fd.description: Join us this Archaeology Week for a family-friendly event offering a unique opportunity to become an archaeologist for the day! Explore the fascinating world of uncovering history.

62204f2d9234daaf.summary: Mandarin Corner workshop on Chinese gold-foil burning art.
62204f2d9234daaf.description: Relaxed meet-up to practice Mandarin with native speakers. Explore the use of gold in Chinese art and create a simple gold-foil design.

62d42dd48958cee6.description: Hackathons offer a platform for students to engage in creative problem-solving with real-world applications. An AUT event for those interested in technology, innovation, and collaborative challenges.

6866a93602bd03ca.summary: Experience Day is about getting a taste of the experience of being a student at Massey University Wellington's College of Creative Arts - Toi Rauwhārangi.
6866a93602bd03ca.description: Experience Day is about getting a taste of the experience of being a student at Massey University Wellington's College of Creative Arts - Toi Rauwhārangi. Join us from 9:30am - 3pm on June 12th for workshops, pizza, student panel talks and more! (Registration will open from 9am) Experience days are free to attend, but registration is essential to secure your spot on your chosen workshop. Please note, you can only attend one workshop on the day, so choose the one you are most excited about!

69655c25a9a89e49.summary: The webinar explores the complex systems of voting and elections within ancient Roman society.
69655c25a9a89e49.description: This discussion examines the intricate political mechanics of voting and elections in ancient Rome. Attendees will learn about the structures that underpinned Roman democratic practices and how participation shaped civic life in the Republic and Empire.

6a637e4eaaedf817.summary: Printopia Festival returns for its fifth edition from 1–3 May 2026, celebrating contemporary printmaking across Aotearoa and the Asia Pacific.
6a637e4eaaedf817.description: Printopia Festival returns for its fifth edition from 1–3 May 2026, celebrating contemporary printmaking across Aotearoa and the Asia Pacific. Now a recognised regional arts event, the Festival presents its signature programme including the country’s largest print fair, live printing, artist talks and hands-on workshops. New for 2026 are curated exhibitions, guided gallery tours and experimental performances that expand how print is experienced. Taking place across four central Auckland venues, Printopia invites audiences to connect, explore and be inspired.
6a637e4eaaedf817.exclude_reason: Not a lecture or talk component

6c5fc1c057bcfa78.summary: Authors discuss writing from the margins and global publishing.
6c5fc1c057bcfa78.description: A conversation with three New Zealand authors exploring themes of writing from the margins, finding readership, and telling Kiwi stories in a globalised publishing market.

6c85bd14c67bf65d.summary: Neuroscientist Emeritus Prof. Cliff Abraham discusses the nerve cell processes behind forming and forgetting memories.
6c85bd14c67bf65d.description: Neuroscientist Emeritus Distinguished Professor Cliff Abraham FRSNZ will discuss the neuroscientific processes that allow our brains to encode and subsequently forget memories. Learn how the nerve cells within our brains facilitate the complex mechanics of human memory.

6cf49b2e5a77f75c.summary: AUT Worldwide is back!!!Mark your calendars – AUT Worldwide is taking over our campuses from 5 to 7 May. With students from nearly 140 countries, AUT is h
6cf49b2e5a77f75c.description: AUT Worldwide is back!!!Mark your calendars – AUT Worldwide is taking over our campuses from 5 to 7 May. With students from nearly 140 countries, AUT is h

6edac8ffc7e3fbf6.summary: Royal Society presents an urgent update on climate change.
6edac8ffc7e3fbf6.description: Join the Royal Society for a critical lecture addressing recent developments in climate science and their implications. Thin Ice 2026 aims to provide an urgent update on our planet's changing climate, highlighting key challenges and potential solutions.

6f04d15e54116813.summary: Doctoral candidates from Business, Economics, and Law compete to present their entire thesis in just 180 seconds.
6f04d15e54116813.description: Doctoral candidates from the Faculty of Business and Economics and the Faculty of Law compete in the Three Minute Thesis (3MT) Heat. This challenge provides an opportunity to showcase research in a dynamic and compelling way, with the chance to earn a place in the University 3MT Final.

6f8154369b41962d.summary: Professor Clare McCann's Inaugural Lecture addresses the influence and potential of speech-language therapists.
6f8154369b41962d.description: This lecture explores the persuasive power inherent in the communication skills of speech-language therapists. It addresses how subtle linguistic techniques can shape belief and behaviour in complex ways. Attendees will gain insight into the art and science of therapeutic communication.

71fe220011968c3a.exclude_reason: Not a lecture or educational talk

7215d531111a9854.summary: NZIIA members attend the national AGM followed by an annual lecture featuring the Minister for Trade and Investment, Hon Todd McClay.
7215d531111a9854.description: The event includes the New Zealand Institute of International Affairs (NZIIA) national Annual General Meeting, featuring an address by NZIIA Chair Dr James Kember. This is followed by the annual lecture delivered by Hon Todd McClay, Minister for Trade and Investment.

7423082faeba0062.summary: Deloitte Techweek26 presents a talk on the trade-off between rapid development and accumulating technical debt.
7423082faeba0062.description: This session addresses the critical architectural dilemma of 'vibe coding' versus accruing technical debt while building fast. Join Deloitte Techweek26 for an exploration of sustainable, rapid development strategies.

77b0a203c71c3323.summary: Canada's High Commissioner discusses Indigenous rights and UNDRIP.
77b0a203c71c3323.description: This meeting will start with an AGM followed by the High Commissioner’s talk on Canada’s approach to Indigenous rights, drawing from his personal and professional experience. The discussion will cover domestic commitments in a broader international context, focusing on cooperation between Indigenous peoples and governments, with special attention to synergies with Aotearoa New Zealand.

7879b554665913d7.summary: Screening of 'Welfare' (1975) with introduction and discussion on Aotearoa's welfare systems.
7879b554665913d7.description: This event features a screening of the film 'Welfare' from 1975, accompanied by an introductory talk and post-screening discussion led by artist Rangi White. The focus is on exploring and critiquing the bureaucratic structures within New Zealand's welfare system.

78c60ae414094338.summary: A quiz event at the Aotearoa Art Fair challenging participants' knowledge of art. The event is hosted by ArtSpace.

7bf84ac607a16c8c.summary: Dr Simon Robb explores law-in-context insights, drawing from his regulatory career, to deepen understanding of governance and regulatory practice.
7bf84ac607a16c8c.description: Dr Simon Robb draws on academic foundations in sociology and law, alongside his regulatory career, to explore insights into law-in-context. This discussion will deepen the understanding of governance and regulatory practice within the legal framework.

7c69365a7120b66f.summary: Dr Oliver Hartwich discusses adapting to a tribal world in crisis.
7c69365a7120b66f.description: In today's complex global landscape marked by five interconnected crises—erosion of trust, decay at the institutional core, complacency about sustaining conditions, retreat from reason, and loss of Western confidence—Dr Oliver Hartwich explores adaptation strategies. He highlights these challenges as part of our current reality rather than future forecasts. Dr Hartwich is Executive Director of The New Zealand Initiative in Wellington.

7c6f453987ef3c34.summary: This event screens 'fugue notes' (2025) by Selina Ershadi, which was remastered for the London BFI Film Festival.
7c6f453987ef3c34.description: The first screening of 'fugue notes' (2025) by Selina Ershadi. This film was included in the Anatomy of Place, Sites of Becoming section of the London BFI Film Festival.

7fd7ba297f4c93f3.summary: Details For many generations, Aotearoa has invested comparatively little, certainly by OECD standards, in research and development (R and D). Moreover, since 2020–21,...

80a8f6a8a9efa1b2.summary: CreativeMornings Auckland returns after a reset period with a new organizing team, fresh themes, and inspiring speakers.

82e172e0631b1028.summary: Join a free, educational deep dive into Aotearoa New Zealand’s founding document, Te Tiriti o Waitangi.
82e172e0631b1028.description: To mark the signing of Te Tiriti o Waitangi (The Raukawa Moana sheet) on 29 April 1840, join us for a free, educational, deep dive into Aotearoa New Zealand’s founding document.

844b9ea53dc84eb6.summary: Mandarin Corner celebrates Dragon Boat Festival, crafts herbal sachets.
844b9ea53dc84eb6.description: Join our Mandarin Corner for a relaxed meet-up to practise Mandarin with native speakers. Celebrate the Dragon Boat Festival and craft your own traditional herbal sachet.

86b784eecf036925.summary: This lecture explores New Zealand's rapidly growing space sector, its science capabilities enabled by domestic launch, and the governing policy frameworks.
86b784eecf036925.description: This exploration covers New Zealand's rapidly growing space sector, the science enabled by domestic launch capability, and the necessary policy frameworks governing the country's use of space.

87635656d44c1448.summary: Discuss the latest in AI-assisted coding at the Prompt Poets Society gathering at 2degrees headquarters.
87635656d44c1448.description: The cogs of AI progress keep a turning! Join the April 2026 Prompt Poets Society meeting at the 2degrees headquarters on Fanshawe St on April 28th at 6:00pm. Attendees will discuss and swap notes on AI-assisted coding in a collegial atmosphere, featuring prepared talks and open discussion. Pizza from Totos and beverages are provided.

87d8cabb11424c41.summary: Experience Chinese shadow puppetry at Victoria University.
87d8cabb11424c41.description: Experience the storytelling magic of traditional Chinese shadow puppetry. Watch a live demonstration, learn about its history and cultural significance.

8b30fa94d015ef93.summary: Film screening of Welfare (1975) with Rangi White's introduction on Aotearoa's welfare bureaucracies.
8b30fa94d015ef93.description: Artist Rangi White introduces a film screening of 'Welfare' (1975), directed by Frederick Wiseman, which explores the complexities and challenges within New Zealand's welfare system. The documentary highlights issues such as housing, unemployment, and medical problems faced by both clients and workers navigating bureaucratic laws.

93d3306be97bd05b.summary: This talk explores the idea that future customers may not be human, hosted by Deloitte Techweek26.
93d3306be97bd05b.description: This talk explores the possibility that future customers may not be human. Hosted by Deloitte Techweek26, the event takes place at Deloitte Auckland on Tuesday, May 19th, 2026.

95282080ccc6256f.summary: Former diplomat Frank Wilson discusses his experiences and the wider regional implications of the Fall of Saigon in 1975.
95282080ccc6256f.description: Frank Wilson, a former senior New Zealand diplomat with a strong career background in Asia, will discuss his personal experiences and those of other New Zealanders during the Fall of Saigon in April 1975. He will explore what this event meant for New Zealand and its wider regional and international implications.

95802a3f9566c770.summary: This lecture examines Shakespeare's role as a reviser through the lens of 'The Merry Wives of Windsor.'
95802a3f9566c770.description: This lecture analyzes the intricate process of revision in Shakespeare's works, using 'The Merry Wives of Windsor' as a prime example. Attendees will gain insight into how Shakespeare reworked existing material, offering a deeper appreciation for the play's dramatic evolution and thematic depth. The examination illuminates revision as a core element of his creative process.

960eb2c54daf54c8.summary: Content Forecast: Blood, violence, depictions of mental distress and death. R13.About the showHamlet’s looking paler than usual. Their father’s dead, mother’s remarried, and...
960eb2c54daf54c8.description: Content Forecast: Blood, violence, depictions of mental distress and death. R13.About the showHamlet’s looking paler than usual. Their father’s dead, mother’s remarried, and...

speakers.name: THEATRE 318 (Te Kāhui: Company) and 319 (Scenography in Produ

968071dd09d57105.summary: Printopia Festival returns for its fifth edition with expanded programmes.
968071dd09d57105.description: Printopia Festival returns for its fifth edition from 1–3 May 2026, celebrating contemporary printmaking across Aotearoa and the Asia Pacific. The Festival presents a signature programme including the largest print fair in the country, live printing, artist talks, and hands-on workshops. New additions for 2026 include curated exhibitions, guided gallery tours, and experimental performances.

97b507e8a2192a3a.summary: Professor Prasanna Gai explores shipping lanes, inflation risk, and optimal monetary policy.
97b507e8a2192a3a.description: Professor Prasanna Gai delves into the relationship between global shipping lanes and economic instability, focusing on hub shocks and their implications for monetary policy. This lecture is part of the Treasury's guest lecture series, featuring expert analysis from leading economists.

9c702e965742d928.summary: The final webinar in the 'This Thinking Life' series examines how things sound historical.
9c702e965742d928.description: This webinar concludes the 'This Thinking Life' series by examining the cultural phenomenon of sounds that evoke historical associations. Participants will explore the mechanisms by which auditory elements become attached to notions of history and time.

a0102426b2daac5e.summary: Professor Rae Frances Irvine explores breakthrough research into the neuroscience of chronic pain and emerging treatments.
a0102426b2daac5e.description: This lecture explores breakthrough research concerning the neuroscience of chronic pain. It examines current understandings of the complex mechanisms underpinning chronic pain and surveys emerging, innovative treatments. The session provides insight into the frontier of pain research.

a244e0dfe41b06b5.summary: Ruth Buchanan guides a tour of The Blue Dome exhibition, providing insight into Selina Ersahdi's new films and installations.
a244e0dfe41b06b5.description: Kaitohu Director Ruth Buchanan guides a tour of the exhibition The Blue Dome. Attendees will gain insight into the newly commissioned films and installation by artist Selina Ersahdi. Ruth will discuss the development of the films.

a2e7b0f7f3d197cd.summary: Ceremony marking the conclusion of teaching programmes.
a2e7b0f7f3d197cd.description: This ceremony marks the completion of Victoria University's Graduate Diploma of Teaching and Master of Teaching and Learning programmes, celebrating the achievements of graduating students.

a38c1adb5dea7997.summary: A full day event for podcast producers, presenters, practitioners, and providers from across Aotearoa, as well as podcast enthusiasts.
a38c1adb5dea7997.description: This summit gathers podcast producers, presenters, practitioners, and providers from across Aotearoa, as well as those curious about podcasting. It promises a full day of collaborative learning and networking for the industry.

a616ebba62bce37b.summary: Webinar discussing NZ's $12B defence commitment and modernisation needs, featuring Major General John Howard and Sir Rod Drury.
a616ebba62bce37b.description: New Zealand has committed $12 billion to defence over four years, with a path to spending more than 2% of GDP within eight years. This webinar launches research on the need for institutional reforms beyond financial investment in modernizing defense capabilities. Speakers will discuss strategic areas like intelligence, space, domestic innovation and digital architecture.

a70971ae735940bb.summary: Bahasa Indonesia, or the language of Indonesia, is spoken by more than 250 million people in Southeast Asia, primarily Indonesia, Malaysia and Singapore.
a70971ae735940bb.description: Bahasa Indonesia, or the language of Indonesia, is spoken by more than 250 million people in Southeast Asia, primarily Indonesia, Malaysia and Singapore. Historically a trading language that linked people across maritime Southeast Asia, contemporary Indonesian is relatively easy to pick up for basic communication purposes, whether you’re planning a holiday in Bali or a business trip to Jakarta.

a7e028a3570d6069.summary: This workshop introduces participants to the world of Chinese martial arts through guided movements, cultural insights, and educational sessions.

aba91cecd08be353.summary: Victoria University hosts linguistics seminar on arguing about morphemes.
aba91cecd08be353.description: The School of Linguistics and Applied Language Studies hosts weekly seminars, featuring a selection of visiting researchers and local experts. This week's topic explores the nuances and debates surrounding morphemes in linguistic theory.

speakers.name: Prof. Laurie Bauer
speakers.bio: School of Linguistics and Applied Language

ac7d494ba6b6c5c1.summary: This webinar explores challenging the patriarchy by reassessing ancient women through academic lenses.
ac7d494ba6b6c5c1.description: This session tackles the concept of patriarchy by re-examining the roles and lives of women throughout ancient history. Participants will gain critical perspectives on how gender structures have been understood and challenged across civilizations.

acb9de0bf9304fb7.summary: This seminar addresses the critical challenge of redefining HR responsibilities in the age of human-machine relationships.
acb9de0bf9304fb7.description: The interplay between human capability and machine intelligence fundamentally changes how organizations manage their workforce. This discussion explores the core principles required to establish ethical and effective relationships between people and technology within HR practices. Attendees will gain insights into redesigning workforce decision-making processes for a hybrid human-machine future.

adb78f3c44febf5d.summary: Join Dr Pilar Villamor for the annual Hochstetter Lecture, as she reviews 25 years of trenching active faults in volcanic environments and how a scientist's perspective on tectonics close to volcanoes changed through that time.
adb78f3c44febf5d.description: Join Dr Pilar Villamor for the annual Hochstetter Lecture, as she reviews 25 years of trenching active faults in volcanic environments and how a scientist's perspective on tectonics close to volcanoes changed through that time.

ae1b385a7cc1d662.summary: This lecture is part of the annual Sir John Graham Lecture series.
ae1b385a7cc1d662.description: The Sir John Graham Lecture provides an annual platform for significant academic discussion. Attendees will engage with a major theme or breakthrough concept presented at the Pullman Auckland Hotel & Apartments. This event offers insight into contemporary intellectual discourse.

af0a10e5eabd00ca.summary: Professor Derick de Jongh's seminar will highlight the distinctive contours of African vs Western leadership theories using Chief Albert Luthuli as a case study.
af0a10e5eabd00ca.description: Derick's seminar will highlight the distinctive contours of African vs Western leadership theories through the case of Chief Albert Luthuli.

b00b8f45e8db70bf.summary: A panel discussion featuring fiction finalists explores the themes, craft, and challenges inherent in writing contemporary New Zealand fiction.
b00b8f45e8db70bf.description: This discussion illuminates the current landscape of New Zealand fiction. Attendees will gain insight into the thematic currents, technical craftsmanship, and creative challenges confronting contemporary authors. The conversation provides a deep look into the state and future direction of the nation's literary output.

b06dbc473ad56b9a.summary: Come join us for a free 45-minute taster class to learn some basics. Learn how to greet, introduce yourself and use some everyday...
b06dbc473ad56b9a.description: Come join us for a free 45-minute taster class to learn some basics. Learn how to greet, introduce yourself and use some everyday...

b3e2988035940c65.summary: The School of Linguistics and Applied Language Studies hosts weekly seminars featuring visiting researchers and those from the department, presented by Dr Mahnaz Aliyar.
b3e2988035940c65.description: The School of Linguistics and Applied Language Studies hosts weekly seminars featuring a selection of visiting researchers as well as those from the department. This seminar is presented by Dr Mahnaz Aliyar, affiliated with LALS, Victoria University of Wellington.

ba2dd526b346cb10.summary: This talk explores how leveraging Artificial Intelligence (AI) can build more resilient value chains within the energy sector.
ba2dd526b346cb10.description: The session examines the crucial role of Artificial Intelligence in fortifying energy value chains against modern disruptions. Attendees will gain insights into practical applications of AI technologies aimed at achieving greater energy resilience across industries. The discussion focuses on how technological advancement meets global energy needs for sustainable operations.

bc36ce59b7e81181.summary: Learn about Kintsugi, the Japanese art of mending broken ceramics with gold, making the object more beautiful than before.
bc36ce59b7e81181.description: Kintsugi, also known as Kintsukuroi, is an age-old Japanese art form. It involves repairing broken ceramics using a method that highlights the breaks with gold lacquer. This philosophy suggests that breakage and repair are part of the history of an object, making it more beautiful than when it was whole.

be71cfa05e7f3e78.summary: A practical nutrition workshop designed to help Auckland University students maintain a healthy diet while studying on campus.

c1776f477373865c.summary: Artist Rangi White introduces a film screening of Frederick Wiseman's Welfare, exploring Aotearoa’s welfare bureaucracies.
c1776f477373865c.description: Artist Rangi White draws on the aesthetics of a Work and Income Office to consider how material and spatial conditions structure a person’s encounters with the state. The event includes a film screening of 'Welfare (1975)' directed by Frederick Wiseman, which explores the complexity of the welfare system.

c18c3e744bc93c82.summary: Examines the history of Redcoat soldiers stationed in New Zealand and across the wider British Empire.
c18c3e744bc93c82.description: This seminar explores the multifaceted history of Redcoat soldiers, detailing their presence in New Zealand and across the broader British Empire. Attendees will gain insight into the military life, social integration, and historical significance of these troops within the colonial context. The discussion centers on primary sources and recent academic interpretations of military history in the Pacific.

c286231c9816d9f2.summary: This webinar explores the intersection of Indigenous psychologies, mainstream psychology, and the Treaty of Waitangi (Te Tiriti).
c286231c9816d9f2.description: This webinar examines the complex relationship between Western psychological frameworks and Indigenous knowledge systems. Participants will gain insight into how Te Tiriti informs the practice and understanding of psychology in Aotearoa New Zealand, fostering a critical dialogue about decolonization within the field.

c2fc63ea6435fa7c.summary: Matthew Hanson will guide attendees through the Artspace Aotearoa championed artists and artworks at the Aotearoa Art Fair.
c2fc63ea6435fa7c.description: Matthew Hanson, Kaiāwhina Whakaaturanga Assistant Curator, will lead an exploration of the artists and artworks featured at the Aotearoa Art Fair that Artspace Aotearoa has supported through its decades of collaboration. Attendees should arrive at 11:15am to receive their headset.

c3fe748234d3e3ec.summary: A free interactive drop-in session for tamariki aged 5 and under. This month learn about sharing stories in New Zealand Sign language.
c3fe748234d3e3ec.description: A free interactive drop-in session for tamariki aged 5 and under. This month learn about sharing stories in New Zealand Sign language.

c6c4b309d0526a61.summary: In celebration of New Zealand Music Month, join Auckland Youth Choir for two special performances in two of the Museum's most iconic spaces: Te Ao Mārama South Atrium, accompanied by the rich tones of the Museum’s Fazioli grand piano, and the stunning acoustics of the Grand Foyer.
c6c4b309d0526a61.description: In celebration of New Zealand Music Month, join Auckland Youth Choir for two special performances in two of the Museum's most iconic spaces: Te Ao Mārama South Atrium, accompanied by the rich tones of the Museum’s Fazioli grand piano, and the stunning acoustics of the Grand Foyer.

c918880121cd1fe6.summary: Lawrence will explore how Europe and New Zealand are strengthening ties in an increasingly interconnected and contested global environment.
c918880121cd1fe6.description: Lawrence will explore how Europe and New Zealand are strengthening ties in an increasingly interconnected and contested global environment. His remarks will highlight the value of working with like-minded partners in the Asia-Pacific region to uphold open, rules-based cooperation and deliver practical outcomes.

ca1f64f253399828.summary: This screening celebrates New Zealand Sign Language week by showcasing the film, Sign of the Times – The Story of New Zealand’s Visual Language.
ca1f64f253399828.description: This screening celebrates New Zealand Sign Language week by presenting the film, Sign of the Times – The Story of New Zealand’s Visual Language. The film provides a look into the history and development of New Zealand’s visual language, offering insight into the cultural significance of sign language within Aotearoa.

cce7967c19d96aa3.summary: A panel discussion on the future of technology in the Marlborough region, hosted by Deloitte Techweek26.
cce7967c19d96aa3.description: This panel discusses the future of technology within the Marlborough region. Hosted by Deloitte Techweek26, the event takes place at Te Au Pūngao in Blenheim.

ce1f249b3c34b63f.summary: The workshop explores the malu, the Samoan female tattoo, as a living archive of cultural knowledge and ancestral presence through embodied practice.
ce1f249b3c34b63f.description: Participants will explore how Indigenous knowledge is encoded in the body, and how these markings intersect with gender, identity, and sovereignty. Grounded in Rosanna Raymond's own malu and artistic practice, the workshop invites critical reflection on cultural appropriation, authorship, and the responsibilities of working with Indigenous visual systems.

ced2707cbf21f28a.summary: Deloitte Techweek26 hosts a presentation on agentic blueprinting to drive value from AI.
ced2707cbf21f28a.description: Deloitte Techweek26 presents on agentic blueprinting, focusing on how to drive value from Artificial Intelligence. The event takes place at Deloitte Wellington on Tuesday, May 19th, 2026.

cee11c9c166f76f7.summary: Explore the world of sharks from prehistoric times to today in a theatrical journey. Dive into deep waters without the need for a wetsuit or breathing apparatus.

d92b21fc2b42ebf3.summary: International Research Symposium on work-integrated learning.
d92b21fc2b42ebf3.description: The International Research Symposium, organized by WACE, explores transforming practice through research in work-integrated education. This event aims to bring together global experts for a collaborative dialogue and innovation in cooperative and experiential educational methods.

dab41faa544e7e75.summary: Demographics, productivity, and AI choices facing NZ will be discussed at an event hosted by Deloitte Techweek26.
dab41faa544e7e75.description: This session hosted by Deloitte Techweek26 examines the critical challenges facing New Zealand, including demographics, productivity levels, and the choices presented by artificial intelligence. The event takes place at Deloitte Wellington on Wednesday, May 20th, 2026.

db21f3bf1d770c7f.summary: Exploring societal challenges in New Zealand.
db21f3bf1d770c7f.description: In Aotearoa, key issues like poverty, inequity, ecological destruction, and climate change are well understood but require urgent action. This lecture delves into these problems and potential solutions for a just society.

speakers.name: Boyd Scirkovich
speakers.bio: Chief Economic Development and Investment Officer

speakers.name: Gareth Hughes
speakers.bio: Director Wellbeing Economy Alliance Aotearoa NZ

speakers.name: Bianca Elkington
speakers.bio: General Manager Education and Employment, Te

speakers.name: Fleur Fitzsimons
speakers.bio: National Secretary Public Service Association Te

speakers.name: Edward Miller
speakers.bio: Researcher, Centre for International Corporate Tax

speakers.name: Rebecca Kiddle and James Berghan
speakers.bio: Director and senior research fellow at

e1cb94aaa2fee747.summary: Victoria hosts a lecture on philanthropy in supply chains.
e1cb94aaa2fee747.description: Victoria University presents 'Philanthropy Along the Supply Chain' by authors Huu Nhan Duong, Emdad Islam, Lubna Rahman, and Cara Vansteenkiste. Abstract focuses on strategic use of donations to trade partners’ affiliated charities.

e2788c45a4a1bec8.summary: Professor Stefano Pampanin discusses lessons learned from the 2011 Canterbury earthquakes regarding building codes and future engineering needs.
e2788c45a4a1bec8.description: Fifteen years after the 2011 Canterbury earthquakes, UC engineers reflect on what was learned, what has changed in global building codes, and what still needs to happen in the field.

e32518eb218ec103.description: Discover how science and creativity intersect through cyanotyping. Experiment with UV light and natural materials to create unique prints under the guidance of Lulu, fostering a relaxed and supportive artistic environment.

e42257690cdde024.summary: Students from the University of Canterbury participate in a Techweek Appathon challenge hosted by Deloitte and ServiceNow.
e42257690cdde024.description: Participants engage in a hands-on Appathon challenge, fostering technological skill-building through the collaboration of Deloitte and ServiceNow. This event provides students with practical experience in developing applications using industry-leading platforms. The challenge format allows attendees to explore the frontiers of digital possibility in a collaborative, applied setting.

e48ddbbc834dc652.summary: Marine scientists will share recent deep-sea research discoveries from the Southern Ocean, covering new species, ecosystem dynamics, and fisheries management implications.
e48ddbbc834dc652.description: Marine scientists will share discoveries from recent voyages to the Southern Ocean. The talk will cover findings related to new species identification, ecosystem dynamics, and the implications these findings hold for fisheries management.

e63364a32a4ba7ef.summary: Attend the NZIIA Auckland Branch AGM and hear Catherine Grant Makokera present on New Zealand’s Trade Policy Amid Global Conflict.
e63364a32a4ba7ef.description: Attend the NZIIA Auckland Branch’s Annual General Meeting for 2026 on Tuesday, April 28th, 2026. The AGM will cover the past year, thank the committee, outline upcoming events, and elect new committee members. Following the AGM, Catherine Grant Makokera will present on New Zealand’s Trade Policy During an Era of Geoeconomics and Global Conflict.

e7ee177f3796e430.summary: Explore the journey, history, and mātauranga surrounding the prestigious pauku cloak at Auckland Museum.
e7ee177f3796e430.description: The Auckland War Memorial Museum invites you to hear about the journey of the pauku, its history, care, and the important mātauranga surrounding this prestigious cloak. Join us for an evening at Tāmaki Paenga Hira.

e885f0a7f721ce37.summary: Researchers and community voices discuss the competing demands on Canterbury's freshwater resources and what a sustainable future looks like for the region.
e885f0a7f721ce37.description: This session brings together researchers and community voices to discuss the competing demands on Canterbury's freshwater resources. Participants will explore what a sustainable future looks like for the region's vital freshwater sources.

e8cbee17043daa26.summary: Workshop on Siva Samoa movement and cultural significance.
e8cbee17043daa26.description: This workshop offers a nurturing journey into Siva Samoa, where participants explore foundational steps and their cultural meanings. Through gentle movements, they experience the connection to nature and community, promoting health and collective wellbeing.

ec949af9f84b83e4.summary: Explore the future of fitness and discover the innovative world of sports technology through a weekend filled with engaging activities.

f3fabdf5c39be407.summary: Artist Selina Ershadi and Kaitohu Director Ruth Buchanan provide an informal introduction to The Blue Dome.
f3fabdf5c39be407.description: Alongside the Aotearoa Art Fair and neighbourhood gallery openings, the gallery will remain open until 8pm. Attendees can join artist Selina Ershadi and Kaitohu Director Ruth Buchanan for drinks and an informal introduction to The Blue Dome and its newly commissioned works.

f575220e54475c4f.description: Join us in celebrating the 50th anniversary of the Bachelor of Social Work qualification with an event series reflecting on the past to inform the present and envision the future.
f575220e54475c4f.exclude_reason: Not a lecture or talk component.

f736386a033bed4f.summary: Helen Howell discusses visual priming and speech perception among Samoan speakers at the School of Linguistics and Applied Language Studies.
f736386a033bed4f.description: The School of Linguistics and Applied Language Studies hosts weekly seminars. Helen Howell, from the University of Potsdam, will present on the topic of visual priming and speech perception among Samoan speakers.

f9522d3572d03fb2.summary: Future Environments Thought Leadership session, in partnership with Whaikaha – Ministry of Disabled People, focused on accessible and inclusive design in
f9522d3572d03fb2.description: Future Environments Thought Leadership session, in partnership with Whaikaha – Ministry of Disabled People, focused on accessible and inclusive design in

speakers.name: Rebecca Elvy
speakers.bio: Deputy Chief Executive Outreach and Innovation

f97d2adb031d11ec.summary: Mahdis Azarmandi presents a lecture on 'Inheriting Revolution—On Iran's question of war and peace,' drawing from her research in Peace and Conflict Studies.
f97d2adb031d11ec.description: Drawing from her research within Peace and Conflict Studies, Mahdis Azarmandi will present a lecture providing insight into the complex context surrounding Iran's question of war and peace, framed by the theme of Inheriting Revolution.

speakers.name: Mahdis Azarmandi
speakers.bio: Research in Peace and Conflict Studies

fdd4532b42c7f1b5.summary: Explore the Business Administration – Postgraduate Certificate in Business and its pathway to an Executive MBA.
fdd4532b42c7f1b5.description: Explore the Business Administration – Postgraduate Certificate in Business and learn how it can serve as a pathway towards an Executive MBA.

fea2f6e6b34612a6.summary: Ruth Buchanan presents a live interpretation of her artwork and published collection Evacuation Tapes, exploring the construction of the self.
fea2f6e6b34612a6.description: Kaitohu Director and artist Ruth Buchanan will present a live interpretation of her artwork and published collection Evacuation Tapes (2019). This spoken word work will explore the construction of the self and the ways in which our bodies and their...

ff8ae22d11fe7fac.summary: Artists and filmmakers Selina Ershadi and Nova Paul lead a workshop for early career artists interested in using film to tell personal stories.
ff8ae22d11fe7fac.description: Artists and filmmakers Selina Ershadi and Nova Paul will host this workshop for early career artists interested in moving image making. The session draws on the artists' interest in film as a key tool to tell personal stories.
